findings.title: Sidebar-mode i overlay lets Enter launch a stage invisibly under a still-open chat
findings.scenario: Wide terminal -> s (chat opens, idle) -> alt+h (sidebar mode) -> i -> Enter: stage_detail_input delegates to on_enter, whose guard checks chat_running() (in_flight only), so the idle open chat does not block. run_stage sets tab=Live but draw_main still renders the chat split, the stage streams invisibly, and chat_unfocused_input swallows the advertised x cancel.

findings.title: Sidebar-mode footer claims 'j/k pipeline' at widths where j/k scrolls the transcript
findings.snippet: " j/k pipeline · l edit input · i stage · ctrl+x cancel · esc close"
findings.scenario: Chat open at term width < 100 (sidebar hidden), alt+h to sidebar mode: the footer - the only in-context documentation, since the chat swallows ? - says 'j/k pipeline', but chat_sidebar_nav scrolls the transcript at this width. Was pinned wrong by the boundary_99_chat snapshot.

findings.title: One-frame-stale term_width after resize lets focus-left land on a not-rendered sidebar
findings.scenario: Terminal at 120 cols (last frame drawn), user resizes to 60; an h keypress batched behind the resize event sees term_width==120, so sidebar_hidden() is false and focus becomes Pipeline with no refusal even though the next frame draws no sidebar. Codex's 'before the first render' variant was refuted (the loop draws before processing input); the resize-batch window was real.

findings.title: Terminals delivering alt+left as split Esc-then-Left still destroy the chat draft
findings.scenario: Focused idle chat with a typed draft; the terminal or a slow SSH link delivers alt+left as a bare Esc event followed by Left. The Esc arm closes the chat, dropping ChatState.input irrecoverably; the trailing Left dispatches FocusLeft on the dashboard. term.rs pushes no kitty/DisambiguateEscapeCodes, so nothing distinguishes a lone ESC from an alt prefix. The emergency_escape chord only covers the coalesced single-event encoding.

findings.title: Sidebar-mode transcript scroll is unclamped above the renderer's extent
findings.scenario: Narrow chat (sidebar hidden), short transcript; hold k 50 times -> chat.scroll=50 while the renderer clamps display at max_start, so the user must press j ~50 times before the tail moves again. Mirrors the pre-existing unclamped focused-chat Up handler; a clamp needs a chat extent field in ViewMax.

findings.title: alt+l/alt+right allegedly fail to move focus out of the focused chat input
findings.scenario: Codex read alt+l/alt+right as leave-chat chords; by design they are focus-right (return to the input from sidebar mode), so from the focused input they are no-ops. Verified in code: alt+l is correctly swallowed by the plain-char guard; the only residue is that alt+right matches the unmodified KeyCode::Right arm and moves the text cursor by one - benign.
